source_title: Learn Web Scraping in 5 Minutes (NO PRIOR KNOWLEDGE)

concepts.name: Web Scraping
concepts.definition: The automated process of extracting data from websites.

concepts.name: HTML Tags
concepts.definition: The building blocks of web pages (e.g., <div>, <a>, <h1>) used to structure content.

concepts.name: HTTP Requests
concepts.definition: The protocol used to fetch data from a server; scraping typically uses the GET method.

steps.explanation: Use browser developer tools to identify the HTML tags and classes containing the desired data.
steps.gotcha: Websites may have different mobile and desktop layouts.

steps.explanation: Download the raw HTML content of the page.
steps.gotcha: The server might block requests that don't include a User-Agent header.

steps.explanation: Convert the raw string into a searchable object structure.
steps.gotcha: Ensure you use the correct parser library (e.g., lxml vs html.parser).

steps.explanation: Find items based on tags, IDs, or class names.
steps.gotcha: Classes with spaces represent multiple classes and must be handled carefully.

code_recipes.name: Minimalist Python Scraper
code_recipes.purpose: Fetch and print titles from a target webpage.
code_recipes.code:
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup

url = 'http://example.com'
response = requests.get(url)
soup = BeautifulSoup(response.text, 'html.parser')

for title in soup.find_all('h1'):
    print(title.get_text())

gotchas.context: When a website uses JavaScript (React/Vue) to load data after the initial page load.
gotchas.solution: Use tools like Selenium or Playwright, or inspect the Network tab for hidden API endpoints.

gotchas.context: Sending too many requests in a short period.
gotchas.solution: Implement delays (time.sleep) or use a proxy rotation service.
Check the website's robots.txt file and Terms of Service before scraping.
